The dominant technical signal is a severe 5-day drawdown of -8.01% with RSI at 64.5 falling through key support, coupled with the price now 14.6% below the 200-day MA — a structural downtrend that outweighs intra-day oversold conditions. On fundamentals, cement sector macro conditions are genuinely strong: Brent crude down 11.4% in 21 days cuts energy and freight costs (+2 sector points), cooling CPI (7.3% YoY) opens the door to rate cuts that release leverage constraints on cement balance sheets (+2 sector points), and copper up 7.1% in 21 days signals robust construction demand (+1 sector point). However, institutional flows are actively negative (big-fish net = -5.3 mn PKR today; mutual funds selling -7.6 mn), suggesting informed money is pulling back despite favorable macro conditions — a powerful warning signal that the sector setup is priced in or that leverage concerns outweigh the rate-cut narrative. The PSX overnight gap prior is FLAT (+0.25%) on stable-VIX conditions, but the market filter is OFF (risk_on=False) and top-5 momentum names are absent, indicating retail and smaller players are in defensive mode.
